name: Gastroenterology and Hepatology
summary: Comprehensive consultative and diagnostic procedures in gastroenterology and liver disease.
description: The Department of Gastroenterology and Hepatology at the Randwick Health Campus provides a comprehensive range of consultative and diagnostic procedures. Specialist diagnostic services include endoscopy and colonoscopy, endoscopic ultrasound, ERCP, capsule endoscopy, and Fibroscan. The department runs weekly outpatient clinics including general gastroenterology, inflammatory bowel disease, and advanced liver disease.
contact_address: Level 2, Prince of Wales Hospital, Barker Street, Randwick NSW 2031
referral_info: Referrals are accepted from GPs and specialists via NSW Health eReferral or fax. Urgent referrals should be marked accordingly and include recent pathology and imaging results.

patient_resources.title: Preparing for your colonoscopy
patient_resources.description: Dietary instructions and bowel preparation guide for patients scheduled for colonoscopy.

patient_resources.title: Inflammatory Bowel Disease patient information
patient_resources.description: Resources for patients living with Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis.

patient_resources.title: Liver disease and Fibroscan information
patient_resources.description: What to expect during a non-invasive liver stiffness scan.

name: Cardiology
summary: Comprehensive cardiac care including imaging, electrophysiology, and heart failure management.
description: The Cardiology Department provides inpatient and outpatient services for patients with heart disease across the Randwick campus. Services include cardiac imaging, electrophysiology, interventional cardiology, heart failure clinics, and cardiac rehabilitation programmes in partnership with the Health Translation Hub.
contact_address: Cardiology Outpatients, Prince of Wales Hospital, Barker Street, Randwick NSW 2031
referral_info: GP and specialist referrals accepted. Emergency cardiac presentations should attend the Prince of Wales Hospital Emergency Department.

patient_resources.title: Heart failure self-management guide
patient_resources.description: Daily monitoring tips, medication information, and when to seek urgent care.

patient_resources.title: Cardiac rehabilitation programme
patient_resources.description: Information about supervised exercise and education sessions after a cardiac event.

name: Mental Health Services
summary: Adult and youth mental health care including emergency, inpatient, and community services.
description: Mental Health Services at the Randwick campus provide acute and community-based care for adults and young people. The service includes a psychiatric emergency centre, inpatient units, youth mental health programmes, and specialised clinics for treatment-resistant depression and mood disorders.
contact_address: Mental Health Services, Prince of Wales Hospital, Barker Street, Randwick NSW 2031
referral_info: Community referrals via GP or mental health triage. For crisis support, contact the NSW Mental Health Line on 1800 011 511.

patient_resources.title: Youth mental health services guide
patient_resources.description: Information for young people and families accessing headspace and hospital youth programmes.

patient_resources.title: Treatment-resistant depression clinic
patient_resources.description: Referral criteria and what to expect from neurostimulation therapies including TMS.

name: Paediatric Services
summary: Specialist medical and surgical care for children and adolescents at Sydney Children's Hospital.
description: Sydney Children's Hospital Randwick provides world-class paediatric care across more than 30 specialty areas. As part of the Sydney Children's Hospitals Network, the service delivers emergency, inpatient, outpatient, and day-stay care for children from across NSW and beyond.
referral_info: Referrals from GPs and paediatricians via SCHN eReferral. Emergency presentations go directly to the Children's Hospital Emergency Department.

patient_resources.title: Your hospital stay — information for families
patient_resources.description: What to bring, visiting hours, and support services available during your child's admission.

patient_resources.title: Rare diseases and genetics programme
patient_resources.description: Information about the precinct's multidisciplinary rare disease clinics.

name: Emergency Medicine
summary: 24/7 emergency care for adults and children at the Randwick Health Campus.
description: The Randwick Health Campus operates separate adult and paediatric emergency departments. Prince of Wales Hospital Emergency provides tertiary-level trauma and medical emergency care. Sydney Children's Hospital Emergency is dedicated to paediatric emergencies and is one of the busiest children's EDs in Australia.
contact_address: Barker Street & High Street, Randwick NSW 2031
referral_info: For life-threatening emergencies call 000. For non-urgent advice contact Healthdirect on 1800 022 222.

patient_resources.title: When to visit the Emergency Department
patient_resources.description: Guidance on when to attend ED versus your GP or after-hours service.

patient_resources.title: Children's Emergency Department guide
patient_resources.description: What to expect when bringing your child to Sydney Children's Hospital Emergency.

name: Medical Oncology
summary: Comprehensive cancer diagnosis, treatment, and supportive care services.
description: The Medical Oncology service at Prince of Wales Hospital provides chemotherapy, immunotherapy, and coordinated multidisciplinary cancer care. The service works closely with radiation oncology, surgical oncology, and the precinct's research institutes to offer patients access to clinical trials.
contact_address: Oncology Day Unit, Level 3, Prince of Wales Hospital, Barker Street, Randwick NSW 2031
referral_info: Referrals accepted from GPs and specialists. Multidisciplinary team review is arranged for all new cancer diagnoses.

patient_resources.title: Cancer treatment — what to expect
patient_resources.description: Overview of chemotherapy, side effect management, and supportive care services.

patient_resources.title: Clinical trials at Randwick
patient_resources.description: Information about open cancer clinical trials available through the RHIP research network.

name: Respiratory and Sleep Medicine
summary: Diagnosis and treatment of lung disease, sleep disorders, and lung transplantation.
description: The Respiratory Medicine department provides comprehensive care for patients with asthma, COPD, interstitial lung disease, sleep apnoea, and advanced pulmonary conditions. The service includes a lung function laboratory and works closely with the precinct lung transplantation programme.
contact_address: Thoracic Medicine, Prince of Wales Hospital, Barker Street, Randwick NSW 2031
referral_info: GP and specialist referrals accepted via NSW Health eReferral. Sleep study referrals require completion of a sleep questionnaire.

patient_resources.title: Sleep apnoea — patient guide
patient_resources.description: Information about CPAP therapy and sleep study preparation.

patient_resources.title: Lung function test preparation
patient_resources.description: Instructions for patients attending the lung function laboratory.

name: Nephrology (Kidney Disorders)
summary: Specialist care for chronic kidney disease, dialysis, and kidney transplantation.
description: The Nephrology service at Prince of Wales Hospital manages the full spectrum of kidney disease from early chronic kidney disease through to dialysis and transplant coordination. Outpatient clinics, dialysis units, and multidisciplinary renal care teams support patients across South Eastern Sydney.
contact_address: Renal Unit, Prince of Wales Hospital, Barker Street, Randwick NSW 2031
referral_info: Referrals from GPs and specialists. Urgent renal referrals should include recent creatinine, eGFR, and urinalysis results.

patient_resources.title: Living with chronic kidney disease
patient_resources.description: Diet, medication, and monitoring guidance for CKD patients.

patient_resources.title: Dialysis — what to expect
patient_resources.description: Information for patients starting haemodialysis or peritoneal dialysis.

name: Rheumatology
summary: Diagnosis and management of arthritis, autoimmune diseases, and musculoskeletal conditions.
description: The Rheumatology department provides outpatient and infusion services for patients with inflammatory arthritis, lupus, vasculitis, and other autoimmune conditions. The service coordinates biologic therapy infusions and works with allied health for comprehensive musculoskeletal care.
contact_address: Rheumatology Outpatients, Prince of Wales Hospital, Barker Street, Randwick NSW 2031
referral_info: Referrals accepted from GPs and specialists. Please include inflammatory markers and relevant imaging with referral.

patient_resources.title: Understanding rheumatoid arthritis
patient_resources.description: Treatment options, lifestyle advice, and monitoring for RA patients.
